name: In Focus: afra eisma
description: Join artist afra eisma, in unpacking the making of warrior garments. Explore the ideas contained within the artworks, what it means to centre sexual violence and personal trauma through art, and the precarity of talking about it across nations and institutions. Discover the contours of afra’s practice, the people and art that inspires them, and the hope they carry for the future in these dark times.

name: Summer at MAP (Ages 8-12)
description:
Spend the summer inside a museum, learning from professional artists. Our workshops for children aged 8–12 are hands-on, creative, and fun, covering everything from tie-dye to ceramics and more. 

Choose your experience

Register for individual workshops, or go all-in with our combination ticket for access to every session throughout the camp.

May 4

Ceramics and Mask Making Workshop

May 5

Anthotype and Cyanotype Workshop

May 6

Canvas City

May 7

Growing From Nothing: A Gardening Walk Workshop

May 8

Natural Tie-Dye Workshop

End-of-camp showcase

The final day is a celebration where the participants present everything they made over the summer, open for all families to attend.

Note: 

-Please pack a lunch, snacks, and a water bottle for your child each day.

-Every registered participant receives a gift store voucher of equal value, redeemable at the MAP Gift Store. 

-If you're a working parent, our café space is open for you to use while your child is in session.

These workshops are for children between the ages of 8-12. If you are between the ages of 13-16 check out our programme here.

Spots are limited. Register early to secure your place.

name: Summer at MAP (Ages 13-16)
description:
Five days, a museum full of inspiration, and a workshop unlike any other, join film maker Hansa Thapliyal for doll-making and stop-motion animation at MAP.

Using the museum as a creative playground, participants will work with everyday materials and scraps to build their own dolls, drawing inspiration from our exhibition Beneath the Turning Sky. Along the way, they'll explore their ideas, experiment with storytelling, and find their own creative voice.

Through playful stop-motion exercises, these ideas will begin to move, building into a collaborative animated film screened at the end of Summer at MAP.

Participants are required to bring:

-A mobile phone with a camera

-A few discarded items from home to add a personal touch to the creations

Note: 

-Please pack a lunch, snacks, and a water bottle for your child each day.

-Every registered participant receives a gift store voucher of equal value, redeemable at the MAP Gift Store. 

-If you're a working parent, our café space is open for you to use while your child is in session.

These workshops are for children between the ages of 13-16 If you are between the ages of 8-12 check out our programme here.

Spots are limited. Register early to secure your place.

name: Deaf Bookwave
description:
Join us for an afternoon of poetry, discussion and learning with anti-caste Dalit poet Daniel Sukumar who will share his work, and delve into the tools he uses to make art. Daniel’s poetry focuses on the living truths and harsh realities of caste in today’s world. They offer social commentary on what it means to simply exist in modern India. If you are looking to explore the art of self, understand caste and social narratives, and its relationship with mental health this is for you. 

The programme is developed in collaboration with Deaf Bookwave, a book-club created by Deaf people, for Deaf people. It is a space where the Deaf community can come together to read, discuss, and express emotions, thoughts, and experiences through books and sign language. Deaf Bookwave believes that Deaf people deserve access to stories, literature, and emotional expression in their own language and culture. The book club encourages Deaf readers to explore different genres, share personal reflections, and connect with others in a fully Deaf-friendly environment. Deaf Bookwave hosts regular book discussions, emotional sharing circles, creative sessions, and more. Every Deaf voice matters, and every story brings us closer together. At Deaf Bookwave, we celebrate Deaf identity, Deaf expression, and the power of storytelling.

name: Mend and Make
description:
This is a hand-stitching workshop guided by artist afra eisma and Karin Iturralde Nurnberg—at the heart of which is the idea of emotional repair.

Patches mend and reinforce worn garments, they can also represent healing, protection, and strength. Drawing on the theme of the warrior garments, participants are encouraged to reflect on their own experiences and emotions, translating them into stitched forms. Through this process, making patches become more than decoration or repair; they become a way of reclaiming, protecting, and reimagining parts of oneself.

name: Learning Lounge
description:
A series of faculty-led sessions in the form of talks, lectures or workshops are delivered by faculty members of Srishti Manipal Institute of Art, Design & Technology. These sessions range from sounds, urban biodiversity, metaphor-making, livelihoods, ecosystems and more.

Schedule
April 7
On Seeing the Sound in a Changing Environment by Dr Guruaja, K. V.

April 14
Where Have all the Sparrows Gone? by Aparna Raman

April 21
Slow Disturbances by Priya Joseph

April 25
Climate Metaphors by Asmita Sarkar

May 5
The Ghosts of Conservation by Rustam Vania

May 9
Climate Canvases by Dr Chandrima Home and Dr Sambita Modak

Please note: Each session requires individual registration. Some sessions require you to bring certain materials so please be prepared.

name: On Seeing the Sound in a Changing Environment
description:
Krause (2013) says "a picture is worth a thousand words, but a soundscape is worth a thousand pictures". This showcases the power of sound in both conveying information and evoking emotion.

In this workshop, we will explore the world of sounds to understand nature-human dynamics in various spatial and temporal aspects.Hearing is different from listening, and we engage in active listening of sound, visualizing them using some free softwares are also part of this workshop. The landscape is changing and so is the time and climate. How our active listening and recording the sound help in understanding these changes and also start creating repositories for endangered sounds. Eg- the sound from Kabadiwala on a Sunday morning or door-to-door playing of Nadaswara in the mornings of Karthika masa could be examples of spatial and temporally embedded sound.

Participants can bring their own laptop with following softwares
https://www.ravensoundsoftware.com/raven-downloads/

Raven Pro ® is free for Indian Citizens
https://www.audacityteam.org/download/

Audacity is a light software for sound visualization
Song meter touch (free app for both Android and iOS) by Wildlife Acoustics ®

This is part of series of events in collaboration with Srishti Manipal Institute of Art, Design & Technology (SMI).
